key_insight: Treating each token as a matrix Lie group element lets attention use the intrinsic relative transform log(g_i^{-1}g_j), yielding a tautologically equivariant, representation-free score.
engineering_relevance: This provides a closed-form attention mechanism for geometric data on SE(2), SO(3), and even non-compact affine groups, with far fewer parameters than learned invariant kernels.
method: Tokens are bare group elements g_i, not feature vectors.
Compute relative pose g_i^{-1}g_j and map it via the Lie algebra logarithm.
Score attention by the negative squared algebra norm: s_ij = -||log(g_i^{-1}g_j)||^2_λ / τ.
evidence_signal: In sequence-completion tests on SE(2), SO(3), and Aff(2), the closed-form score matched a learned MLP kernel on the same invariant, beat it on SE(2), used 50–80x fewer score parameters, and preserved invariance far better than a vector-token baseline.
watch_item: The method depends on a logarithm chart that contains the relative poses, so practical use requires staying within the valid chart domain and handling non-compact group numerics carefully.
paper_title: The Token Is a Group Element: On Lie-Algebra Attention over Matrix Lie Groups
